_description: Audit log tampering: a row in the entries table has a prev_hash value that does NOT match the entry_hash of the chronologically prior entry. This simulates a file-level tamper that bypassed the append-only SQLite triggers (e.g., via PRAGMA writable_schema=ON). The photophore.audit.AuditLog.verify_chain() method MUST detect this and return (False, tampered_entry_id). Behavioral wire: tests/test_audit_chain_property.py (AUDIT-08, Hypothesis property test, >=100 examples).

scenario.description: Two audit entries; second entry has a corrupted prev_hash.
scenario.tamper_method: PRAGMA writable_schema=ON; DROP TRIGGER entries_no_update; UPDATE entries SET prev_hash='deadbeef...' WHERE id=entry_2_id; PRAGMA writable_schema=OFF
scenario.detection_method: AuditLog.verify_chain() re-walks the chain, checks each entry's prev_hash against the previous entry's entry_hash

notes: The '(real hash ...)' placeholders are intentional. Phase 4 conformance harness (CONF-04) computes deterministic hashes from test vectors. Phase 2 ships the wire-shape contract. The Hypothesis property test in test_audit_chain_property.py exhaustively tests both payload and prev_hash tamper paths with >=100 generated cases.
